summary.text: The dominant pattern today is the sudden breakout of 'addyosmani/agent-skills', which gained 2,051 stars in 24 hours with 4x acceleration, reflecting intense developer interest in structured, reusable skill packs for AI coding agents. This is reinforced by the sustained hot streak of 'x1xhlol/system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools' (139k stars, 2.8x acceleration) and the continued growth of 'nexu-io/open-design' and 'rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch', both of which package expert knowledge into agent-ready formats. Meanwhile, the MCP/tool-calling category shows strong weekly momentum (avg 2,673 stars/week) but no breakout today, suggesting steady adoption rather than a spike. The absence of new product launches on Product Hunt and the Hacker News focus on Claude Fable 5 (2,547 votes) indicate that developer attention is split between practical agent tooling and frontier model releases. Looking forward, the convergence of skill-pack repositories and system-prompt collections may indicate a maturing ecosystem where the bottleneck shifts from agent capability to expert knowledge encoding.

projects.name: agent-skills
projects.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A production-grade pack of 24 structured skills and slash commands that encode senior engineering workflows for AI coding agents.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality output because they lack the structured workflows, quality gates, and best practices that senior engineers follow. This repository packages those practices into reusable, agent-executable skills.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has exploded from 0 to 51,894 stars with a 24h delta of 2,051 and a 7d delta of 3,844. The acceleration ratio is 4 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is not gradual growth — it's a viral spike driven by the release of a comprehensive, well-documented skill pack for multiple agent platforms.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The repository was created recently and has seen a rapid initial burst of stars. The README describes a full set of 24 skills and 7 slash commands, with setup instructions for Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot, Kiro, and Codex. The sudden breakout suggests a launch announcement or viral social media post by the author (Addy Osmani, a well-known Google Chrome engineer) or coverage on Hacker News / Twitter.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses a pain point for developers using AI coding agents: inconsistent output quality. By providing ready-made, production-grade skills that work across multiple agent platforms, it lowers the barrier to adopting structured agent workflows. The author's reputation and the comprehensive documentation accelerate adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a practical, immediately usable resource for any developer using AI coding agents. You can install it in minutes and get consistent, senior-engineer-level behavior from your agent across the entire development lifecycle.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a shift from ad-hoc agent prompting to structured, skill-based agent programming. It suggests that the future of AI-assisted development lies in reusable, verifiable skill packs rather than raw LLM prompts. Developers should consider adopting or contributing to such skill ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Copilot, etc.) who want to enforce consistent, high-quality engineering practices without manual prompting.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The skills are opinionated and may not fit every team's workflow. Over-reliance on predefined skills could reduce flexibility. The project is very new (no release history), so long-term maintenance and community adoption are unproven.

projects.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
projects.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go with prefix-cache stability for low-cost long sessions.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Reduces token costs and latency for AI-assisted coding by optimizing around DeepSeek's prefix cache, providing a config-driven, plugin-extensible terminal agent that stays efficient over long-running sessions.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 20,849 stars, a 24-hour delta of +492, and a 7-day delta of +3,475, the project shows sustained high velocity. The acceleration ratio of 0.7 indicates steady, organic growth rather than a sudden spike, making it a reliable signal of ongoing community interest.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 1.0 ground-up rewrite in Go (from TypeScript) was released recently, as indicated by the README and the 'hasRecentRelease' flag. This major version change likely attracted attention from developers seeking better performance and lower costs.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It taps into the growing demand for cost-effective AI coding agents, specifically optimized for DeepSeek's models, which are known for competitive pricing. The Go rewrite promises better performance and a single-binary distribution, appealing to developers who value simplicity and speed.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This project demonstrates how to build a terminal-based AI agent that leverages model-specific optimizations (prefix caching) to reduce operational costs, a pattern that could be applied to other LLMs.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, this signals a shift toward specialized, model-native agents that optimize for specific provider strengths (e.g., DeepSeek's cache). It also highlights the value of config-driven, plugin-extensible architectures for building versatile coding tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers who use DeepSeek models for coding assistance, especially those working in terminal environments and seeking a lightweight, cost-effective alternative to heavier IDE plugins.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-cache behavior; changes to DeepSeek's pricing or caching policies could impact cost benefits. The project is still relatively new (1.0 rewrite), and the high number of open issues (339) may indicate growing pains or active development churn.

projects.name: open-design
projects.description: 🎨 Local-first, open-source Claude Design alternative. 🖥️ Native desktop app. ⚡ 259+ Skills · ✨ 142+ Design Systems 🖼️ Web · desktop · mobile prototypes · slides · images · videos · HyperFrames 📦 Sandboxed preview · HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export 🤖 Claude Code / OpenClaw / Codex / Cursor / OpenCode / Qwen / Copilot / Hermes / Kimi & 17+ CLIs.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your coding agent into a design engine with 150+ design systems, 260+ plugins, and multi-format export.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Design tools are either closed-source, cloud-dependent, or disconnected from the AI coding workflow. Open Design bridges the gap by letting developers use their existing coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) to generate prototypes, decks, images, and videos directly from a local design system, all without leaving the terminal or IDE.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 62,959 stars, a 24-hour delta of +569, and a 7-day average of +959 stars per day, Open Design is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 0.5 indicates steady momentum rather than a sudden spike. The recent 0.9.0 release (within 30 days) and active commits within the last 7 days show the project is actively maintained and improving. This is a project that has already captured significant developer mindshare and continues to grow.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 0.9.0 release introduced an official Model Router built into the app, removing the need for manual API key setup. This lowers the barrier to entry significantly, likely driving the recent uptick in adoption. The README also highlights a new 'Open Design Fellow' program, which may be fostering community engagement.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines two hot trends: local-first AI tools and agent-native design workflows. Developers want to stay in their coding environment (CLI, IDE) and avoid switching to a separate design tool. Open Design lets them generate production-ready artifacts using the same agents they already use for coding, with a rich ecosystem of design systems and plugins.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you're building AI-powered design or prototyping tools, Open Design sets a new baseline for what developers expect: local-first, agent-agnostic, and extensible via plugins and MCP. The project's rapid growth signals strong demand for tools that integrate design into the AI coding loop.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The shift from pixel-pushing to agent-native design is real. Open Design's architecture — skills, design systems, plugins, and MCP server — is a blueprint for how AI tools can compose with existing developer workflows. The BYOK proxy and support for 21+ CLIs mean it's not locked to any one model provider, which is a key adoption driver.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and designers who use AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) and want to generate UI prototypes, presentations, images, and videos without leaving their terminal or IDE. Also relevant for teams that want to enforce brand consistency via DESIGN.md files.
llmSummary.riskNotes: As an open-source project with rapid growth, there may be scalability challenges in maintaining quality across 260+ plugins and 150+ design systems. The project is still in early stages (0.9.0), so breaking changes are possible. Dependency on multiple third-party AI APIs could introduce reliability issues.

projects.name: ai-engineering-from-scratch
projects.description: Learn it. Build it. Ship it for others.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production-grade agents, shipping a reusable artifact per lesson.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-only. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path that builds deep understanding by implementing algorithms from scratch before using frameworks, closing the gap between AI tool usage and professional readiness.

llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The high star velocity over 7 days suggests ongoing word-of-mouth and social sharing rather than a single event.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It addresses the pain point that 84% of students use AI tools but only 18% feel prepared professionally. The curriculum's scope (503 lessons, 4 languages, 20 phases) and its 'build from scratch' philosophy resonate with developers who want genuine understanding. The built-in agent skills (/find-your-level, /check-understanding) and reusable artifacts (prompts, skills, agents, MCP servers) make it immediately useful.

It's not just a tutorial — it's a structured curriculum that produces a portfolio of 503 artifacts. The inclusion of Rust, Julia, and TypeScript alongside Python broadens its appeal.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The market is shifting from API-wrapping to deep understanding. Developers who can build from scratch and ship production artifacts will have a significant advantage. This repo signals that the community values rigorous, hands-on learning over quick demos.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and engineers who want to understand AI deeply, from math to production. Students preparing for AI careers. Professionals transitioning into AI engineering. Anyone tired of surface-level tutorials and wanting to build real, shippable AI systems.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The curriculum is massive (320 hours), which may overwhelm some users. It's a solo project with no recent release, so long-term maintenance depends on the creator. The 'from scratch' approach may be too slow for those needing quick deployment skills.

projects.name: code-review-graph
projects.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first code intelligence graph that slashes AI token usage by ~82x median during code reviews via MCP and CLI.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding tools waste tokens by re-reading entire codebases on every review task. code-review-graph builds a persistent, incremental AST graph (Tree-sitter + SQLite) that computes the minimal blast radius of changes, so AI assistants read only relevant files.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 18,337 stars, a 24h delta of +46, and a 7-day average of +76.2 stars/day, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.6 (moderate) indicates steady organic interest rather than a viral spike, making it a reliable tool for the AI coding ecosystem.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits (within 7 days) and a release within 30 days suggest active maintenance, and the README mentions broad platform support (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) that likely drives ongoing adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses the token-cost pain point for AI-assisted development, offers a local-first privacy guarantee, and integrates seamlessly with major AI coding tools via MCP. The benchmarked 38x–528x token reduction is a compelling, quantifiable value proposition.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) on medium-to-large repos, this tool can dramatically cut token costs and speed up reviews. The one-command install and GitHub Action make it easy to adopt.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local-first, context-aware AI tooling that minimizes API costs and latency. Developers building AI coding agents should consider integrating similar graph-based context pruning to stay competitive.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) on codebases of any size, especially monorepos or projects with frequent code reviews.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Project is relatively new (v2.x) with 122 open issues; dependency on Tree-sitter grammars may limit language coverage for niche languages. The 0.71 average impact F1 suggests room for improvement in relevance accuracy.

projects.name: ECC
projects.description: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A harness-native operator system that optimizes AI agent performance across multiple coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) with skills, memory, security, and continuous learning.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using AI coding agents face fragmented workflows, inconsistent performance, and lack of cross-harness optimization. ECC provides a unified system of skills, hooks, memory persistence, and security scanning that works across multiple agent harnesses, reducing token waste and improving agent reliability.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 212,749 stars and a sustained 7-day average of 1,604 new stars per day, ECC is one of the fastest-growing developer tools on GitHub. The 24-hour delta of 835 stars and acceleration of 0.6 (high) indicate continued strong organic interest. The recent v2.0.0 release (June 2026) with 261 skills, operator workflows, and a Rust control-plane alpha likely fueled the latest surge.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The v2.0.0 stable release (June 2026) introduced 261 skills, the control-pane substrate, worktree-lifecycle service, and orchestrator family. The v2.0.0-rc.1 (April 2026) added a dashboard GUI, operator workflows, prediction-market skills, and optimization packs. These releases expanded the system's scope from a config pack to a full agent operating system.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ECC addresses a critical pain point for the growing number of developers using AI coding agents: inconsistent behavior across different tools. By providing a cross-harness optimization layer, it reduces friction and improves productivity. The MIT license, active community (230+ contributors), and regular releases build trust and momentum.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: ECC is a must-evaluate tool for any developer or team using AI coding assistants. It offers immediate productivity gains through token optimization, memory persistence, and security scanning, while its open-source nature allows customization. The cross-harness support future-proofs against tool switching.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of ECC signals a maturing AI developer tool ecosystem where users demand consistent, optimized experiences across multiple agents. This trend suggests that standalone agent configurations are insufficient; developers want integrated systems that manage the entire agent lifecycle. The opportunity lies in building complementary tools or plugins that integrate with ECC's skill and hook system.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini, Zed, GitHub Copilot) who want to optimize agent performance, reduce token costs, and maintain consistent workflows across different harnesses.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is maintained by a single developer (affaan-m), which creates bus-factor risk despite the large community. The rapid growth may lead to maintenance challenges and feature bloat. Some users may find the system complex to set up, though the guides aim to mitigate this.

watchlistMovements.name: agent-skills
watchlistMovements.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A production-grade pack of 24 structured skills and slash commands that encode senior engineering workflows for AI coding agents.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality output because they lack the structured workflows, quality gates, and best practices that senior engineers follow. This repository packages those practices into reusable, agent-executable skills.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has exploded from 0 to 51,894 stars with a 24h delta of 2,051 and a 7d delta of 3,844. The acceleration ratio is 4 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is not gradual growth — it's a viral spike driven by the release of a comprehensive, well-documented skill pack for multiple agent platforms.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The repository was created recently and has seen a rapid initial burst of stars. The README describes a full set of 24 skills and 7 slash commands, with setup instructions for Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot, Kiro, and Codex. The sudden breakout suggests a launch announcement or viral social media post by the author (Addy Osmani, a well-known Google Chrome engineer) or coverage on Hacker News / Twitter.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses a pain point for developers using AI coding agents: inconsistent output quality. By providing ready-made, production-grade skills that work across multiple agent platforms, it lowers the barrier to adopting structured agent workflows. The author's reputation and the comprehensive documentation accelerate adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a practical, immediately usable resource for any developer using AI coding agents. You can install it in minutes and get consistent, senior-engineer-level behavior from your agent across the entire development lifecycle.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a shift from ad-hoc agent prompting to structured, skill-based agent programming. It suggests that the future of AI-assisted development lies in reusable, verifiable skill packs rather than raw LLM prompts. Developers should consider adopting or contributing to such skill ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Copilot, etc.) who want to enforce consistent, high-quality engineering practices without manual prompting.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The skills are opinionated and may not fit every team's workflow. Over-reliance on predefined skills could reduce flexibility. The project is very new (no release history), so long-term maintenance and community adoption are unproven.

watchlistMovements.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
watchlistMovements.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go with prefix-cache stability for low-cost long sessions.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Reduces token costs and latency for AI-assisted coding by optimizing around DeepSeek's prefix cache, providing a config-driven, plugin-extensible terminal agent that stays efficient over long-running sessions.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 20,849 stars, a 24-hour delta of +492, and a 7-day delta of +3,475, the project shows sustained high velocity. The acceleration ratio of 0.7 indicates steady, organic growth rather than a sudden spike, making it a reliable signal of ongoing community interest.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 1.0 ground-up rewrite in Go (from TypeScript) was released recently, as indicated by the README and the 'hasRecentRelease' flag. This major version change likely attracted attention from developers seeking better performance and lower costs.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It taps into the growing demand for cost-effective AI coding agents, specifically optimized for DeepSeek's models, which are known for competitive pricing. The Go rewrite promises better performance and a single-binary distribution, appealing to developers who value simplicity and speed.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This project demonstrates how to build a terminal-based AI agent that leverages model-specific optimizations (prefix caching) to reduce operational costs, a pattern that could be applied to other LLMs.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, this signals a shift toward specialized, model-native agents that optimize for specific provider strengths (e.g., DeepSeek's cache). It also highlights the value of config-driven, plugin-extensible architectures for building versatile coding tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers who use DeepSeek models for coding assistance, especially those working in terminal environments and seeking a lightweight, cost-effective alternative to heavier IDE plugins.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-cache behavior; changes to DeepSeek's pricing or caching policies could impact cost benefits. The project is still relatively new (1.0 rewrite), and the high number of open issues (339) may indicate growing pains or active development churn.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your coding agent into a design engine with 150+ design systems, 260+ plugins, and multi-format export.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Design tools are either closed-source, cloud-dependent, or disconnected from the AI coding workflow. Open Design bridges the gap by letting developers use their existing coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) to generate prototypes, decks, images, and videos directly from a local design system, all without leaving the terminal or IDE.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 62,959 stars, a 24-hour delta of +569, and a 7-day average of +959 stars per day, Open Design is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 0.5 indicates steady momentum rather than a sudden spike. The recent 0.9.0 release (within 30 days) and active commits within the last 7 days show the project is actively maintained and improving. This is a project that has already captured significant developer mindshare and continues to grow.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 0.9.0 release introduced an official Model Router built into the app, removing the need for manual API key setup. This lowers the barrier to entry significantly, likely driving the recent uptick in adoption. The README also highlights a new 'Open Design Fellow' program, which may be fostering community engagement.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines two hot trends: local-first AI tools and agent-native design workflows. Open Design lets them generate production-ready artifacts using the same agents they already use for coding, with a rich ecosystem of design systems and plugins.

The project's rapid growth signals strong demand for tools that integrate design into the AI coding loop.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The shift from pixel-pushing to agent-native design is real. Open Design's architecture — skills, design systems, plugins, and MCP server — is a blueprint for how AI tools can compose with existing developer workflows. The BYOK proxy and support for 21+ CLIs mean it's not locked to any one model provider, which is a key adoption driver.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and designers who use AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) and want to generate UI prototypes, presentations, images, and videos without leaving their terminal or IDE. Also relevant for teams that want to enforce brand consistency via DESIGN.md files.
llmSummary.riskNotes: As an open-source project with rapid growth, there may be scalability challenges in maintaining quality across 260+ plugins and 150+ design systems. The project is still in early stages (0.9.0), so breaking changes are possible. Dependency on multiple third-party AI APIs could introduce reliability issues.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production-grade agents, shipping a reusable artifact per lesson.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-only. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path that builds deep understanding by implementing algorithms from scratch before using frameworks, closing the gap between AI tool usage and professional readiness.

llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. llmSummary.whyTrending: It addresses the pain point that 84% of students use AI tools but only 18% feel prepared professionally. The curriculum's scope (503 lessons, 4 languages, 20 phases) and its 'build from scratch' philosophy resonate with developers who want genuine understanding. The built-in agent skills (/find-your-level, /check-understanding) and reusable artifacts (prompts, skills, agents, MCP servers) make it immediately useful.

llmSummary.riskNotes: The curriculum is massive (320 hours), which may overwhelm some users. It's a solo project with no recent release, so long-term maintenance depends on the creator. The 'from scratch' approach may be too slow for those needing quick deployment skills.

watchlistMovements.name: code-review-graph
watchlistMovements.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first code intelligence graph that slashes AI token usage by ~82x median during code reviews via MCP and CLI.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding tools waste tokens by re-reading entire codebases on every review task. code-review-graph builds a persistent, incremental AST graph (Tree-sitter + SQLite) that computes the minimal blast radius of changes, so AI assistants read only relevant files.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 18,337 stars, a 24h delta of +46, and a 7-day average of +76.2 stars/day, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.6 (moderate) indicates steady organic interest rather than a viral spike, making it a reliable tool for the AI coding ecosystem.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits (within 7 days) and a release within 30 days suggest active maintenance, and the README mentions broad platform support (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) that likely drives ongoing adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses the token-cost pain point for AI-assisted development, offers a local-first privacy guarantee, and integrates seamlessly with major AI coding tools via MCP. The benchmarked 38x–528x token reduction is a compelling, quantifiable value proposition.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) on medium-to-large repos, this tool can dramatically cut token costs and speed up reviews. The one-command install and GitHub Action make it easy to adopt.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local-first, context-aware AI tooling that minimizes API costs and latency. Developers building AI coding agents should consider integrating similar graph-based context pruning to stay competitive.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) on codebases of any size, especially monorepos or projects with frequent code reviews.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Project is relatively new (v2.x) with 122 open issues; dependency on Tree-sitter grammars may limit language coverage for niche languages. The 0.71 average impact F1 suggests room for improvement in relevance accuracy.

watchlistMovements.name: ECC
watchlistMovements.description: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A harness-native operator system that optimizes AI agent performance across multiple coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) with skills, memory, security, and continuous learning.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using AI coding agents face fragmented workflows, inconsistent performance, and lack of cross-harness optimization. ECC provides a unified system of skills, hooks, memory persistence, and security scanning that works across multiple agent harnesses, reducing token waste and improving agent reliability.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 212,749 stars and a sustained 7-day average of 1,604 new stars per day, ECC is one of the fastest-growing developer tools on GitHub. The 24-hour delta of 835 stars and acceleration of 0.6 (high) indicate continued strong organic interest. The recent v2.0.0 release (June 2026) with 261 skills, operator workflows, and a Rust control-plane alpha likely fueled the latest surge.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The v2.0.0 stable release (June 2026) introduced 261 skills, the control-pane substrate, worktree-lifecycle service, and orchestrator family. The v2.0.0-rc.1 (April 2026) added a dashboard GUI, operator workflows, prediction-market skills, and optimization packs. These releases expanded the system's scope from a config pack to a full agent operating system.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ECC addresses a critical pain point for the growing number of developers using AI coding agents: inconsistent behavior across different tools. By providing a cross-harness optimization layer, it reduces friction and improves productivity. The MIT license, active community (230+ contributors), and regular releases build trust and momentum.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: ECC is a must-evaluate tool for any developer or team using AI coding assistants. It offers immediate productivity gains through token optimization, memory persistence, and security scanning, while its open-source nature allows customization. The cross-harness support future-proofs against tool switching.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of ECC signals a maturing AI developer tool ecosystem where users demand consistent, optimized experiences across multiple agents. This trend suggests that standalone agent configurations are insufficient; developers want integrated systems that manage the entire agent lifecycle. The opportunity lies in building complementary tools or plugins that integrate with ECC's skill and hook system.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini, Zed, GitHub Copilot) who want to optimize agent performance, reduce token costs, and maintain consistent workflows across different harnesses.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is maintained by a single developer (affaan-m), which creates bus-factor risk despite the large community. The rapid growth may lead to maintenance challenges and feature bloat. Some users may find the system complex to set up, though the guides aim to mitigate this.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Career-Ops turns any AI coding CLI into a job search command center, automating offer evaluation, CV tailoring, and portal scanning.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Job seekers waste time manually tracking applications and tailoring resumes. Career-Ops automates the pipeline: evaluates offers with a structured scoring system, generates ATS-optimized PDFs, scans job portals, and tracks everything in a single source of truth.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 52,503 stars, a 24-hour delta of 793, and a 7-day average of 662.8 stars per day, Career-Ops is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The acceleration of 0.8 indicates steady, high-velocity attention, not a sudden spike. It's trending because it directly addresses the pain of modern job hunting with a practical, AI-powered tool that has proven results (740+ evaluations, 100+ CVs, one dream role landed).
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release v1.9.0 and continuous commits (last push June 10, 2026) keep the project active, but the star velocity has been consistently high for weeks, suggesting word-of-mouth and media coverage (WIRED, Business Insider) are driving sustained interest.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Career-Ops solves a universal pain point (job searching) with a novel approach: using AI coding CLIs (Claude Code, Gemini CLI) as the interface. It's open-source, well-documented, and has a compelling creator story. The combination of practical utility, AI hype, and a polished README with demo GIFs and press badges makes it highly shareable.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This project demonstrates how to build a practical AI agent that integrates with existing developer tools (CLIs, Playwright) to automate a complex workflow. It's a blueprint for 'AI workflow automation' tools that augment rather than replace human decision-making.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Career-Ops signals a shift toward AI-powered personal productivity tools that leverage coding CLIs as general-purpose agent runtimes. Developers should watch for more 'agentic CLI' patterns that combine LLM reasoning with browser automation and local file generation.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Job seekers in tech (especially AI/ML roles), career changers, and anyone overwhelmed by the application process. Also developers interested in building AI agents that interact with web portals and generate documents.

modelDemoSignals.title: Towards Responsibly Non-Compliant Machines
modelDemoSignals.description: We consider the problem of engineering autonomous intelligent agents that are capable to responsibly not comply with user requests. We argue that machine non-compliance comes in many different forms, and sketch the issues we should pursue on the road of accomplishing responsibly non-compliant intelligent machines. We anchor responsible non-compliance in justifications for task refusal, pathways to override the non-compliance, as well as careful tracking of security risks and liability transfers.

modelDemoSignals.title: nD-RoPE: A Generalized RoPE for n-Dimensional Position Embedding
modelDemoSignals.description: Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) is widely adopted in Transformer models, yet its extension to high-dimensional domains lacks a unified theoretical formulation. Most existing approaches either apply rotations independently along each axis or empirically mix frequencies, which limits cross-dimensional interactions and yields direction-dependent representations. To address these limitations, we propose nD-RoPE, a decomposition-free generalization of RoPE to arbitrary dimensions. From a translation
modelDemoSignals.summary: Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) is widely adopted in Transformer models, yet its extension to high-dimensional domains lacks a unified theoretical formulation. Most existing approaches either apply rotations independently along each axis or empirically mix frequencies, which limits cross-dimensional interactions and yields direction-dependent representations. To address these limitations, we propose nD-RoPE, a decomposition-free generalization of RoPE to arbitrary dimensions. From a translation-invariant formulation in continuous Hilbert space, we derive a spectral condition for isotropy that requires treating positions and frequencies as coupled \(n\)-dimensional vectors. We instantiate this formulation with a multi-scale regular-simplex wave-vector design, which provides non-degenerate spatial coverage and a symmetric, directionally balanced second-order response. Experiments across images, videos, and point clouds demonstrate consistent performance gains and improved generalization in high-dimensional settings.
modelDemoSignals.author: Boyang Li, Yulin Wu, Sizhe Xu, Nuoxian Huang, Zhonghang Yuan, Shangyi Guo, Shu Yang, Takahiro Yabe
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, embedding

modelDemoSignals.title: Soft-Prompt Tuning for Fair and Efficient LLM Benchmark Evaluation
modelDemoSignals.description: Benchmark scores often misrepresent a large language model's (LLM's) knowledge, because they rely, e.g., on the model's ability to follow specific formatting requirements. This especially penalizes base models that may know the correct answers but lack the ability -- typically introduced in post-training -- to structure them as instructed. To overcome this, we propose soft-prompt tuning, an efficient, fair, and architecture-agnostic model evaluation. By optimizing only 10 soft-prompt vectors (ro
modelDemoSignals.summary: Benchmark scores often misrepresent a large language model's (LLM's) knowledge, because they rely, e.g., on the model's ability to follow specific formatting requirements. This especially penalizes base models that may know the correct answers but lack the ability -- typically introduced in post-training -- to structure them as instructed. To overcome this, we propose soft-prompt tuning, an efficient, fair, and architecture-agnostic model evaluation. By optimizing only 10 soft-prompt vectors (roughly 0.0006% parameters for a 7B model) over a short tuning period, we adapt models to specific benchmark formats, closing gaps in format-following and ensuring that underlying knowledge is accurately reflected in benchmark scores. This allows one to fairly compare different base models -- trained with various pre-training recipes -- on benchmarks without the need for full post-training. We evaluated soft-prompt tuning across 7 models and 7 datasets. The results show that (a) soft-prompt tuning saturates format-following within 80 steps (~640 samples) making it highly efficient, (b) soft-prompt tuning significantly outperforms zero- and few-shot prompting, surfacing base model knowledge that standard prompting misses, that (c) even post-trained models can benefit from soft-prompts to maximize format compliance, and that (d) soft-prompted base model performance predicts post-trained model rankings more reliably than zero- and few-shot baselines, offering a low-cost proxy for downstream model quality. Our contributions include (1) metrics which disentangle format-following and knowledge accuracy, (2) a fairer benchmarking protocol of LLM knowledge, and (3) a cost- and memory-effective recipe to identify optimal pre-training strategies early in LLM development.
modelDemoSignals.author: Selen Erkan, Bastian Boll, Kristian Kersting, Björn Deiseroth, Letitia Parcalabescu
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, benchmark, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.title: Detecting Sensitive Personal Information in Japanese Pre-Training Corpora for Large Language Models
modelDemoSignals.description: Sensitive personal information can appear in large-scale pre-training corpora for large language models (LLMs). Detecting and filtering such information is therefore essential to ensure compliance with privacy regulations and prevent unintended information leakage. However, in contrast to English and other languages, research into sensitive personal information has been limited in the Japanese language. In this study, we focus on sensitive personal data defined as special care-required personal
modelDemoSignals.summary: Sensitive personal information can appear in large-scale pre-training corpora for large language models (LLMs). Detecting and filtering such information is therefore essential to ensure compliance with privacy regulations and prevent unintended information leakage. However, in contrast to English and other languages, research into sensitive personal information has been limited in the Japanese language. In this study, we focus on sensitive personal data defined as special care-required personal information (SCPI) under Japan's Act on the Protection of Personal Information (APPI). We construct an SCPI dataset using LLM-based annotation and train machine learning models to rapidly detect SCPI in text. As a result, our SCPI classifier can effectively identify information related to SCPI. This study is the first to explore SCPI detection in Japanese text corpora, highlighting the challenges of accurate detection.
modelDemoSignals.author: Rei Minamoto, Yusuke Oda, Daisuke Kawahara
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

modelDemoSignals.title: Augmenting Molecular Language Models with Local $n$-gram Memory
modelDemoSignals.description: Transformer-based language models for SMILES strings suffer from a locality gap: standard character-level tokenization fragments chemically meaningful motifs, forcing models to repeatedly learn local syntax at the expense of long-range dependencies. To address this without disrupting standard tokenizers, we propose MolGram, which integrates a conditional $n$-gram memory module into molecular language models. MolGram maps local string patterns to learned embeddings via scalable hash lookups and d
modelDemoSignals.summary: Transformer-based language models for SMILES strings suffer from a locality gap: standard character-level tokenization fragments chemically meaningful motifs, forcing models to repeatedly learn local syntax at the expense of long-range dependencies. To address this without disrupting standard tokenizers, we propose MolGram, which integrates a conditional $n$-gram memory module into molecular language models. MolGram maps local string patterns to learned embeddings via scalable hash lookups and dynamically injects this regional context into hidden states. Evaluations across three tasks, including unconditional molecule generation, forward reaction prediction, and single-step retrosynthesis, show that MolGram consistently improves performance. Crucially, our analyses demonstrate that MolGram outperforms baselines with 3$\times$ more parameters, establishing explicit local pattern memory as a highly efficient inductive bias.
modelDemoSignals.author: Xinni Zhang, Zijing Liu, He Cao, Yu Li, Irwin King

aihotHighlights.title: mlx-vlm v0.6.3 发布，Day-0 支持 Google DeepMind DiffusionGemma 和 Cohere North Mini Code 1.0
aihotHighlights.summary: mlx-vlm v0.6.3 上线，首发支持 DiffusionGemma 和 North Mini Code 1.0。DiffusionGemma 采用全新架构：以 256 token 块为单位并行生成、双向注意力、迭代自纠错；26B MoE 仅激活 3.8B，量化后 18GB 即可运行。North Mini Code 1.0 为 30B MoE，仅激活 3B，BF16 下约 66 tok/s。两款模型均通过深度合作实现 Day-0 MLX 支持，可在 Mac 本地运行。可通过 `uv pip install -U mlx-vlm` 安装体验。

aihotHighlights.title: Google Research提出审计机器遗忘新框架
aihotHighlights.summary: Google Research 在 AISTATS 2026 发表正则化 f-散度核检验，用于高效审计 LLM 等模型的机器遗忘。该方法通过统计两样本检验判断模型是否真正"忘记"特定训练数据，避免完全重训的巨大成本。相比最大均值差异等现有工具，新框架理论上可在任意样本量下自然控制假阳性，且假阴性风险随可用样本增加可靠收敛至零，解决了大规模模型审计中计算成本过高的问题。

aihotHighlights.title: DiffusionGemma：文本生成速度提升4倍的开源扩散模型
aihotHighlights.summary: Google DeepMind 发布开源实验模型 DiffusionGemma，采用文本扩散技术，突破自回归逐 token 生成方式，每次前向并行生成 256 个 token。该 26B MoE 模型推理时仅激活 3.8B 参数，量化后适配 18GB 显存消费级 GPU。在 H100 上达 1000+ tokens/s，RTX 5090 上 700+ tokens/s，速度提升 4 倍。具备双向注意力和自我修正能力，面向内联编辑、代码填充等本地交互工作流，以 Apache 2.0 许可证开放。

aihotHighlights.title: Google将保存用户的Lens图片、Search Live录音和Translate音频用于AI训练
aihotHighlights.summary: Google更新搜索交互数据保存方式，新增"Search Services History"设置，用于保存用户搜索时使用的图片、文件、音频和视频，包括Google Lens搜索的图片、实时搜索工具Search Live的录音、语音搜索和Translate中的语音片段。这些数据将被用于"提供、改进和开发AI模型"。用户可关闭该设置并禁用"Save Media"选项以避免保存。

llmSummary.whatItIs: Apache Burr is an open-source framework for building reliable AI agents and applications, recently accepted into the Apache Software Foundation. It provides tools for designing, developing, and deploying trustworthy agentic systems with a focus on reliability, observability, and production readiness.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project gained significant developer attention on Hacker News (174 upvotes, 91 comments) and was featured on AI Hot, indicating strong interest in frameworks that address reliability and observability in AI agent development. The Apache branding adds credibility and signals potential for community-driven growth.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: AI/ML engineers building production agent systems, platform teams evaluating agent frameworks, and developers interested in open-source AI infrastructure.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Addresses the need for reliable AI agents in enterprise applications, potentially reducing risk and improving trust in AI-driven workflows. Apache governance may encourage adoption in risk-averse organizations.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a structured approach to building agents with built-in observability and reliability patterns, potentially reducing boilerplate and debugging effort.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Early-stage project; actual production adoption and community maturity are unproven. Hacker News attention does not guarantee sustained development or widespread use.

items.title: mlx-vlm v0.6.3 发布，Day-0 支持 Google DeepMind DiffusionGemma 和 Cohere North Mini Code 1.0
items.description: mlx-vlm v0.6.3 上线，首发支持 DiffusionGemma 和 North Mini Code 1.0。DiffusionGemma 采用全新架构：以 256 token 块为单位并行生成、双向注意力、迭代自纠错；26B MoE 仅激活 3.8B，量化后 18GB 即可运行。North Mini Code 1.0 为 30B MoE，仅激活 3B，BF16 下约 66 tok/s。两款模型均通过深度合作实现 Day-0 MLX 支持，可在 Mac 本地运行。可通过 `uv pip install -U mlx-vlm` 安装体验。

items.title: Google Research提出审计机器遗忘新框架
items.description: Google Research 在 AISTATS 2026 发表正则化 f-散度核检验，用于高效审计 LLM 等模型的机器遗忘。该方法通过统计两样本检验判断模型是否真正"忘记"特定训练数据，避免完全重训的巨大成本。相比最大均值差异等现有工具，新框架理论上可在任意样本量下自然控制假阳性，且假阴性风险随可用样本增加可靠收敛至零，解决了大规模模型审计中计算成本过高的问题。

items.title: Apache Burr：构建可靠的人工智能代理和应用程序
items.description: Apache Burr 是一个用于构建可靠 AI 智能体和应用程序的框架，已在 Apache 基金会下发布。该项目提供工具和抽象，帮助开发者设计、开发和部署可信任的智能体应用，强调可靠性、可观测性和生产级部署能力。

mlx-vlm v0.6.3 发布，Day-0 支持 Google DeepMind DiffusionGemma 和 Cohere North Mini Code 1.0
items.description: mlx-vlm v0.6.3 上线，首发支持 DiffusionGemma 和 North Mini Code 1.0。DiffusionGemma 采用全新架构：以 256 token 块为单位并行生成、双向注意力、迭代自纠错；26B MoE 仅激活 3.8B，量化后 18GB 即可运行。North Mini Code 1.0 为 30B MoE，仅激活 3B，BF16 下约 66 tok/s。两款模型均通过深度合作实现 Day-0 MLX 支持，可在 Mac 本地运行。可通过 `uv pip install -U mlx-vlm` 安装体验。

llmSummary.whatItIs: A CLI proxy that intercepts LLM API calls and reduces token usage by 60-90% via caching, deduplication, and compression, implemented as a single Rust binary with zero dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: Sustained high star growth (2,630 stars in 7 days) and a high heat score (420) indicate strong developer interest in cost optimization for LLM usage, especially as token costs remain a pain point.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers building AI-powered tools, DevOps engineers managing LLM API costs, and product managers seeking to reduce operational expenses for AI features.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly addresses the growing need to control LLM API costs, which can be a significant expense for AI-integrated products. Potential to reduce cloud spend without sacrificing functionality.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Offers a drop-in solution to cut token usage with minimal integration effort. Developers can test it quickly via CLI to see immediate cost savings.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Stars and delta reflect attention, not production adoption. Effectiveness depends on usage patterns; may not work equally for all LLM calls. Single-binary approach may limit extensibility.

trendEntities.title: mlx-vlm v0.6.3 发布，Day-0 支持 Google DeepMind DiffusionGemma 和 Cohere North Mini Code 1.0
normalizedKeys: title:mlx vlm v0 6 3 发布 day 0 支持 google deepmind diffusiongemma 和 cohere north mini code 1 0

items.title: mlx-vlm v0.6.3 发布，Day-0 支持 Google DeepMind DiffusionGemma 和 Cohere North Mini Code 1.0
items.summary: mlx-vlm v0.6.3 上线，首发支持 DiffusionGemma 和 North Mini Code 1.0。DiffusionGemma 采用全新架构：以 256 token 块为单位并行生成、双向注意力、迭代自纠错；26B MoE 仅激活 3.8B，量化后 18GB 即可运行。North Mini Code 1.0 为 30B MoE，仅激活 3B，BF16 下约 66 tok/s。两款模型均通过深度合作实现 Day-0 MLX 支持，可在 Mac 本地运行。可通过 `uv pip install -U mlx-vlm` 安装体验。

items.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei 发文呼吁缩小AI政策差距
items.summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei 今日发布新文《Policy on the AI Exponential》，指出AI发展极快，远超现有政策制定流程的应对能力。文章阐述了当前技术所处阶段，并列举缩小这一差距所需的行动。Anthropic 同步宣布启动三项新举措，以支持其CEO提出的框架。

trendEntities.title: Google Research提出审计机器遗忘新框架
normalizedKeys: title:google research提出审计机器遗忘新框架

items.title: Google Research提出审计机器遗忘新框架
items.summary: Google Research 在 AISTATS 2026 发表正则化 f-散度核检验，用于高效审计 LLM 等模型的机器遗忘。该方法通过统计两样本检验判断模型是否真正"忘记"特定训练数据，避免完全重训的巨大成本。相比最大均值差异等现有工具，新框架理论上可在任意样本量下自然控制假阳性，且假阴性风险随可用样本增加可靠收敛至零，解决了大规模模型审计中计算成本过高的问题。

trendEntities.title: 豆包AI误导用户损失600元，还帮用户起诉自己
normalizedKeys: title:豆包ai误导用户损失600元 还帮用户起诉自己

items.title: 豆包AI误导用户损失600元，还帮用户起诉自己
items.summary: 2026年5月，河北李先生向字节跳动旗下月活超3亿的AI聊天机器人豆包咨询退票费，豆包错误回答不到100元，实际退票花费600元。李先生质问后，豆包切换为消费者权益倡导者角色，生成补偿承诺书承诺退还600元但未兑现，后改口称AI无法转账。李先生决定起诉，豆包建议无需律师并帮他起草起诉状。5月12日李先生在北京互联网法院起诉豆包。该案例暴露AI在非技术用户信任导向下的误导与责任困境。

trendEntities.title: 小米发布 MiMo Code V0.1 开源终端 AI 编程助手
normalizedKeys: title:小米发布 mimo code v0 1 开源终端 ai 编程助手

items.title: 小米发布 MiMo Code V0.1 开源终端 AI 编程助手
items.summary: 小米推出开源终端 AI 编程助手 MiMo Code V0.1，附带限时免费使用的多模态模型 MiMo V2.5，支持百万 token 上下文窗口。核心特性包括：无限上下文（自动知识积累与无损压缩）、Agent-模型深度协同（测试-审查-验证闭环）、Compose 模式（规格→计划→构建→报告）、自进化系统、语音输入（基于 MiMo-V2.5-ASR）、兼容 Claude Code（零成本迁移），以及 MIT 许可、支持 Anthropic、OpenAI、DeepSeek、Kimi、GLM 等主流模型提供商。

items.title: Anthropic 研究：AI 数小时内即可从安全补丁构建漏洞利用
items.summary: Anthropic 安全团队发现，其 Mythos Preview AI 模型能在几小时内将 Firefox 和 Windows 内核的安全补丁转化为可工作的漏洞利用，成本仅需数千美元，且无需专业知识。在微软自动更新到达任何设备之前，该模型已完成 8 条完整攻击链。Anthropic 认为传统的补丁节奏已经过时。
items.description: Anthropic 安全团队发现，其 Mythos Preview AI 模型能在几小时内将 Firefox 和 Windows 内核的安全补丁转化为可工作的漏洞利用，成本仅需数千美元，且无需专业知识。在微软自动更新到达任何设备之前，该模型已完成 8 条完整攻击链。Anthropic 认为传统的补丁节奏已经过时。

items.title: 突发：Google 因模型幻觉被判负有法律责任
items.summary: 一项法律裁决判定 Google 对其 AI 模型产生的幻觉内容负有法律责任。该判决可能产生巨大影响，尤其若其他国家跟进做出类似裁定。

trendEntities.title: DiffusionGemma：文本生成速度提升4倍的开源扩散模型
normalizedKeys: title:diffusiongemma 文本生成速度提升4倍的开源扩散模型

items.title: DiffusionGemma：文本生成速度提升4倍的开源扩散模型
items.summary: Google DeepMind 发布开源实验模型 DiffusionGemma，采用文本扩散技术，突破自回归逐 token 生成方式，每次前向并行生成 256 个 token。该 26B MoE 模型推理时仅激活 3.8B 参数，量化后适配 18GB 显存消费级 GPU。在 H100 上达 1000+ tokens/s，RTX 5090 上 700+ tokens/s，速度提升 4 倍。具备双向注意力和自我修正能力，面向内联编辑、代码填充等本地交互工作流，以 Apache 2.0 许可证开放。
items.description: Google DeepMind 发布开源实验模型 DiffusionGemma，采用文本扩散技术，突破自回归逐 token 生成方式，每次前向并行生成 256 个 token。该 26B MoE 模型推理时仅激活 3.8B 参数，量化后适配 18GB 显存消费级 GPU。在 H100 上达 1000+ tokens/s，RTX 5090 上 700+ tokens/s，速度提升 4 倍。具备双向注意力和自我修正能力，面向内联编辑、代码填充等本地交互工作流，以 Apache 2.0 许可证开放。

trendEntities.title: Google将保存用户的Lens图片、Search Live录音和Translate音频用于AI训练
normalizedKeys: title:google将保存用户的lens图片 search live录音和translate音频用于ai训练

items.title: Google将保存用户的Lens图片、Search Live录音和Translate音频用于AI训练
items.summary: Google更新搜索交互数据保存方式，新增"Search Services History"设置，用于保存用户搜索时使用的图片、文件、音频和视频，包括Google Lens搜索的图片、实时搜索工具Search Live的录音、语音搜索和Translate中的语音片段。这些数据将被用于"提供、改进和开发AI模型"。用户可关闭该设置并禁用"Save Media"选项以避免保存。
items.description: Google更新搜索交互数据保存方式，新增"Search Services History"设置，用于保存用户搜索时使用的图片、文件、音频和视频，包括Google Lens搜索的图片、实时搜索工具Search Live的录音、语音搜索和Translate中的语音片段。这些数据将被用于"提供、改进和开发AI模型"。用户可关闭该设置并禁用"Save Media"选项以避免保存。

trendEntities.title: 毕业典礼频现"谈 AI 色变"，微软总裁史密斯呼吁行业必须回应公众担忧
normalizedKeys: title:毕业典礼频现 谈 ai 色变 微软总裁史密斯呼吁行业必须回应公众担忧

items.title: 毕业典礼频现"谈 AI 色变"，微软总裁史密斯呼吁行业必须回应公众担忧
items.summary: 近几周多场毕业典礼上，演讲者宣传AI技术时遭学生嘘声。普林斯顿应届毕业生曾否决一款疑似借助AI设计的毕业典礼夹克。微软总裁布拉德·史密斯回应称，行业必须严肃可信地回答问题。史密斯主张AI应增强人而非取代人，认为实用AI渗透经济的速度可能比行业乐观预期更慢。微软今年计划投入约1900亿美元资本支出，主要用于数据中心。微软AI负责人穆斯塔法·苏莱曼修正此前"大多数白领工作18个月内自动化"的说法，表示仅指AI执行单项任务的能力。上月微软CFO艾米·胡德在杜克大学演讲全程未提AI，未遭嘘声。

trendEntities.title: Cursor Bugbot 更新：速度提升超 3 倍、成本降低 22%、发现更多 Bug
normalizedKeys: title:cursor bugbot 更新 速度提升超 3 倍 成本降低 22 发现更多 bug

items.title: Cursor Bugbot 更新：速度提升超 3 倍、成本降低 22%、发现更多 Bug
items.summary: Cursor 的代码审查工具 Bugbot 迎来重大更新：运行速度提升超 3 倍，成本降低 22%，每轮审查多发现 10% 的 bug，90% 的运行在三分钟内完成。新增 `/review` 命令，可在推送代码前运行 Bugbot 和安全审查，并与 GitHub/GitLab 同步--若已通过 `/review` 审查过同一 diff，打开 PR 时 Bugbot 会自动跳过并备注。支持配置仅审查 PR 中新增内容。性能提升源于驱动 Bugbot 的 Composer 2.5 模型训练改进。Bugbot 遵循模型阻止列表，若组织禁用 Composer 2.5 则自动回退。该功能已在 Cursor 3.7+ 和 cursor.com/agents 上线，CLI 支持即将推出。

trendEntities.title: 百度百舸联合复旦提出LU-KV框架，被ICML 2026录用
normalizedKeys: title:百度百舸联合复旦提出lu kv框架 被icml 2026录用

items.title: 百度百舸联合复旦提出LU-KV框架，被ICML 2026录用
items.summary: 百度百舸团队与复旦大学合作提出Long-horizon Utility KV（LU-KV）框架，将头级KV Cache预算分配建模为面向长程边际效用的全局组合优化问题。LU-KV通过离线画像估计注意力头边际贡献曲线，结合凸包松弛与基于边际效用的贪心求解器，在较低开销下得到接近最优的预算配置，可适配SnapKV、KeyDiff等多类压缩方法。在LongBench和RULER基准上，80%压缩比下性能损失小，降低显存占用和推理延迟。相关论文被ICML 2026录用。

items.title: 华为云发布全球首个端到端具身AI平台CloudRobo
items.summary: 华为云推出全球首个端到端具身AI开发平台CloudRobo，覆盖从数据、模型到部署、集成的全生命周期，基于PB级可信数据底座。在INSPIRE2026上，国家地方共建人形机器人创新中心、Yijiahe Technology、上海交通大学展示了其核心能力：数据与模型双评估系统、主动力控模型快速组装、机器人小时级上云、模型分钟级部署。

trendEntities.title: 谷歌 DeepMind 经济学家伊马斯：尚未发现 AI 造成岗位流失的证据，跟风裁员恐适得其反
normalizedKeys: title:谷歌 deepmind 经济学家伊马斯 尚未发现 ai 造成岗位流失的证据 跟风裁员恐适得其反

items.title: 谷歌 DeepMind 经济学家伊马斯：尚未发现 AI 造成岗位流失的证据，跟风裁员恐适得其反
items.summary: 谷歌 DeepMind AGI 经济学负责人亚历克斯·伊马斯表示，目前没有看到白领岗位因 AI 大规模消失的证据。他强调，若企业因"不裁员就等于 AI 转型慢"的叙事而跟风裁员，可能适得其反。伊马斯认为，AI 更多是接手部分任务、提升生产力，让员工专注机器无法完成的工作，岗位冲击尚未真正出现。

trendEntities.title: 摩尔线程开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，9B/27B 参数基于国产 GPU 全链路训练
normalizedKeys: title:摩尔线程开源 musacoder 代码大模型 9b 27b 参数基于国产 gpu 全链路训练

items.title: 摩尔线程开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，9B/27B 参数基于国产 GPU 全链路训练
items.summary: 摩尔线程发布并开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，含 9B 和 27B 两个参数规模，是业内首个基于国产 GPU 算力底座完成全链路训练与验证的开源模型。后训练流程在基于 MTT S5000 的夸娥智算集群上完成，支持从 PyTorch 标准算子自动生成高性能 CUDA/MUSA 原生 Kernel 代码。在 KernelBench 评测中，MusaCoder-27B-RL 以 Overall Pass@8 93.2%、Avg.@8 88.60% 超越 Claude Opus、DeepSeek-V4 Pro、GLM-5.1、Kimi K2.6 等主流 SOTA 代码模型。

trendEntities.title: 工信部印发《"人工智能+信息通信"创新发展实施意见》
normalizedKeys: title:工信部印发 人工智能 信息通信 创新发展实施意见

items.title: 工信部印发《"人工智能+信息通信"创新发展实施意见》
items.summary: 工信部发文，要求加快建设400Gbps/800Gbps骨干传输网络，优化东中西部国家枢纽节点间通道；推进城域400Gbps及以上、全光交叉等高速光传输系统应用，构建城域毫秒级低时延入算能力。同时推动5G-A/6G、新一代光网络、"IPv6+"、工业互联网与AI融合发展，攻关空口智能化、网络高等级自智、智能体互联网等核心技术。鼓励基础电信企业用AI赋能传统业务，深化智慧个人助理、智慧管家、家庭看护、3D观影等新型应用。

trendEntities.title: 用好 Claude Design 的一些经验
normalizedKeys: title:用好 claude design 的一些经验

items.title: 用好 Claude Design 的一些经验
items.summary: 宝玉分享了5点心得：1. 加入设计系统（如 Adobe Spectrum 2）可避免 AI 味，设为默认后可专注布局与交互。2. 先搭建少量功能，再通过左侧聊天框逐步调整。3. 用 Markup 框选局部评论，Edit 可手动调整元素树。4. 注意上下文管理，新任务创建新会话。5. 通过 Tweaks 面板调整主题、布局、加载状态，也可添加导航快速切换界面。

items.title: 亚马逊的大规模扁平化数据中心网络
items.summary: 亚马逊分享了在大规模数据中心中实现扁平化网络架构的工程实践与设计考量，重点论述了如何通过简化拓扑和路由策略来支撑超大规模集群的高带宽、低延迟通信。文章未披露具体模型或评测数据。

trendEntities.title: 2026年高考，跟着千问，选好志愿！
normalizedKeys: title:2026年高考 跟着千问 选好志愿

items.title: 2026年高考，跟着千问，选好志愿！
items.summary: 千问发布国内首个全周期高考志愿填报Agent，由数百位资深高报师参与训练。该智能体提供AI志愿报告，为考生量身定制深度全面的填报方案；AI志愿日历帮助制定专属填报计划；高考专业知识库整合夸克高考8年积累，并引入志愿专家顾问，数据权威可信赖，全程陪伴考生完成志愿填报。

trendEntities.title: Towards Responsibly Non-Compliant Machines

items.title: Towards Responsibly Non-Compliant Machines
items.description: We consider the problem of engineering autonomous intelligent agents that are capable to responsibly not comply with user requests. We argue that machine non-compliance comes in many different forms, and sketch the issues we should pursue on the road of accomplishing responsibly non-compliant intelligent machines. We anchor responsible non-compliance in justifications for task refusal, pathways to override the non-compliance, as well as careful tracking of security risks and liability transfers.

normalizedKeys: title:nd rope a generalized rope for n dimensional position embedding

items.title: nD-RoPE: A Generalized RoPE for n-Dimensional Position Embedding
items.description: Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) is widely adopted in Transformer models, yet its extension to high-dimensional domains lacks a unified theoretical formulation. Most existing approaches either apply rotations independently along each axis or empirically mix frequencies, which limits cross-dimensional interactions and yields direction-dependent representations. To address these limitations, we propose nD-RoPE, a decomposition-free generalization of RoPE to arbitrary dimensions. From a translation
items.summary: Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) is widely adopted in Transformer models, yet its extension to high-dimensional domains lacks a unified theoretical formulation. Most existing approaches either apply rotations independently along each axis or empirically mix frequencies, which limits cross-dimensional interactions and yields direction-dependent representations. To address these limitations, we propose nD-RoPE, a decomposition-free generalization of RoPE to arbitrary dimensions. From a translation-invariant formulation in continuous Hilbert space, we derive a spectral condition for isotropy that requires treating positions and frequencies as coupled \(n\)-dimensional vectors. We instantiate this formulation with a multi-scale regular-simplex wave-vector design, which provides non-degenerate spatial coverage and a symmetric, directionally balanced second-order response. Experiments across images, videos, and point clouds demonstrate consistent performance gains and improved generalization in high-dimensional settings.
items.author: Boyang Li, Yulin Wu, Sizhe Xu, Nuoxian Huang, Zhonghang Yuan, Shangyi Guo, Shu Yang, Takahiro Yabe
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, embedding

trendEntities.title: Soft-Prompt Tuning for Fair and Efficient LLM Benchmark Evaluation

items.title: Soft-Prompt Tuning for Fair and Efficient LLM Benchmark Evaluation
items.description: Benchmark scores often misrepresent a large language model's (LLM's) knowledge, because they rely, e.g., on the model's ability to follow specific formatting requirements. This especially penalizes base models that may know the correct answers but lack the ability -- typically introduced in post-training -- to structure them as instructed. items.summary: Benchmark scores often misrepresent a large language model's (LLM's) knowledge, because they rely, e.g., on the model's ability to follow specific formatting requirements. This especially penalizes base models that may know the correct answers but lack the ability -- typically introduced in post-training -- to structure them as instructed. To overcome this, we propose soft-prompt tuning, an efficient, fair, and architecture-agnostic model evaluation. By optimizing only 10 soft-prompt vectors (roughly 0.0006% parameters for a 7B model) over a short tuning period, we adapt models to specific benchmark formats, closing gaps in format-following and ensuring that underlying knowledge is accurately reflected in benchmark scores. This allows one to fairly compare different base models -- trained with various pre-training recipes -- on benchmarks without the need for full post-training. We evaluated soft-prompt tuning across 7 models and 7 datasets. The results show that (a) soft-prompt tuning saturates format-following within 80 steps (~640 samples) making it highly efficient, (b) soft-prompt tuning significantly outperforms zero- and few-shot prompting, surfacing base model knowledge that standard prompting misses, that (c) even post-trained models can benefit from soft-prompts to maximize format compliance, and that (d) soft-prompted base model performance predicts post-trained model rankings more reliably than zero- and few-shot baselines, offering a low-cost proxy for downstream model quality. Our contributions include (1) metrics which disentangle format-following and knowledge accuracy, (2) a fairer benchmarking protocol of LLM knowledge, and (3) a cost- and memory-effective recipe to identify optimal pre-training strategies early in LLM development.
items.author: Selen Erkan, Bastian Boll, Kristian Kersting, Björn Deiseroth, Letitia Parcalabescu
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, benchmark, evaluation

trendEntities.title: Detecting Sensitive Personal Information in Japanese Pre-Training Corpora for Large Language Models
normalizedKeys: title:detecting sensitive personal information in japanese pre training corpora for large language models

items.title: Detecting Sensitive Personal Information in Japanese Pre-Training Corpora for Large Language Models
items.description: Sensitive personal information can appear in large-scale pre-training corpora for large language models (LLMs). Detecting and filtering such information is therefore essential to ensure compliance with privacy regulations and prevent unintended information leakage. However, in contrast to English and other languages, research into sensitive personal information has been limited in the Japanese language. In this study, we focus on sensitive personal data defined as special care-required personal
items.summary: Sensitive personal information can appear in large-scale pre-training corpora for large language models (LLMs). Detecting and filtering such information is therefore essential to ensure compliance with privacy regulations and prevent unintended information leakage. However, in contrast to English and other languages, research into sensitive personal information has been limited in the Japanese language. In this study, we focus on sensitive personal data defined as special care-required personal information (SCPI) under Japan's Act on the Protection of Personal Information (APPI). We construct an SCPI dataset using LLM-based annotation and train machine learning models to rapidly detect SCPI in text. As a result, our SCPI classifier can effectively identify information related to SCPI. This study is the first to explore SCPI detection in Japanese text corpora, highlighting the challenges of accurate detection.
items.author: Rei Minamoto, Yusuke Oda, Daisuke Kawahara
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

normalizedKeys: title:augmenting molecular language models with local n gram memory

items.title: Augmenting Molecular Language Models with Local $n$-gram Memory
items.description: Transformer-based language models for SMILES strings suffer from a locality gap: standard character-level tokenization fragments chemically meaningful motifs, forcing models to repeatedly learn local syntax at the expense of long-range dependencies. To address this without disrupting standard tokenizers, we propose MolGram, which integrates a conditional $n$-gram memory module into molecular language models. MolGram maps local string patterns to learned embeddings via scalable hash lookups and d
items.summary: Transformer-based language models for SMILES strings suffer from a locality gap: standard character-level tokenization fragments chemically meaningful motifs, forcing models to repeatedly learn local syntax at the expense of long-range dependencies. To address this without disrupting standard tokenizers, we propose MolGram, which integrates a conditional $n$-gram memory module into molecular language models. MolGram maps local string patterns to learned embeddings via scalable hash lookups and dynamically injects this regional context into hidden states. Evaluations across three tasks, including unconditional molecule generation, forward reaction prediction, and single-step retrosynthesis, show that MolGram consistently improves performance. Crucially, our analyses demonstrate that MolGram outperforms baselines with 3$\times$ more parameters, establishing explicit local pattern memory as a highly efficient inductive bias.
items.author: Xinni Zhang, Zijing Liu, He Cao, Yu Li, Irwin King
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, embedding, evaluation

trendEntities.title: Bridging the Morphology Gap: Adapting VLA Models to Dexterous Manipulation via Intent-Conditioned Fine-Tuning
normalizedKeys: title:bridging the morphology gap adapting vla models to dexterous manipulation via intent conditioned fine tuning

items.title: Bridging the Morphology Gap: Adapting VLA Models to Dexterous Manipulation via Intent-Conditioned Fine-Tuning
items.description: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have demonstrated remarkable zero-shot generalization in robotic manipulation, yet the vast majority of pre-trained pipelines remain strictly confined to low-DoF parallel grippers. Adapting these rich semantic priors to high-DoF dexterous hands introduces a severe morphology gap, direct end-to-end joint fine-tuning inherently causes catastrophic forgetting of spatial reasoning and acute action manifold collapse due to data scarcity. In this paper, we present I
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have demonstrated remarkable zero-shot generalization in robotic manipulation, yet the vast majority of pre-trained pipelines remain strictly confined to low-DoF parallel grippers. Adapting these rich semantic priors to high-DoF dexterous hands introduces a severe morphology gap, direct end-to-end joint fine-tuning inherently causes catastrophic forgetting of spatial reasoning and acute action manifold collapse due to data scarcity. In this paper, we present InDex, a novel, data-efficient adaptation framework rooted in cross-morphology semantic inheritance. Rather than discarding the pre-trained 1-DoF parallel grasp output, we repurpose it as a continuous, macroscopic virtual grasp intent proxy to sequentialize the control topology. We implement a two-stage decoupled learning architecture: the first stage parameter-efficiently aligns the VLA backbone to predict continuous arm trajectories and the scalar grasp intent; the second stage freezes this spatial backbone and leverages an intent-conditioned denoising diffusion head to decode fine-grained joint articulations for multi-fingered end-effectors. Extensive simulation benchmarks across a suite of multi-stage, contact-rich dexterous manipulation tasks demonstrate that InDex effectively masters intricate skills with minimal demonstration data, substantially outperforming monolithic baselines while preserving the robust spatial generalizability of the original VLA prior.
items.author: Chuanke Pang, Junyi Huang, Zhijun Zhao, Yaobing Wang, Kun Xu, Xilun Ding
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, fine-tuning, benchmark

Extensive simulation benchmarks across a suite of multi-stage, contact-rich dexterous manipulation tasks demonstrate that InDex effectively masters intricate skills with minimal demonstration data, substantially outperforming monolithic baselines while preserving the robust spatial generalizability of the original VLA prior.
sourceItems.author: Chuanke Pang, Junyi Huang, Zhijun Zhao, Yaobing Wang, Kun Xu, Xilun Ding
sourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, fine-tuning, benchmark

trendEntities.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have demonstrated remarkable zero-shot generalization in robotic manipulation, yet the vast majority of pre-trained pipelines remain strictly confined to low-DoF parallel grippers. Adapting these rich semantic priors to high-DoF dexterous hands introduces a severe morphology gap, direct end-to-end joint fine-tuning inherently causes catastrophic forgetting of spatial reasoning and acute action manifold collapse due to data scarcity. In this paper, we present InDex, a novel, data-efficient adaptation framework rooted in cross-morphology semantic inheritance. Rather than discarding the pre-trained 1-DoF parallel grasp output, we repurpose it as a continuous, macroscopic virtual grasp intent proxy to sequentialize the control topology. We implement a two-stage decoupled learning architecture: the first stage parameter-efficiently aligns the VLA backbone to predict continuous arm trajectories and the scalar grasp intent; the second stage freezes this spatial backbone and leverages an intent-conditioned denoising diffusion head to decode fine-grained joint articulations for multi-fingered end-effectors. Extensive simulation benchmarks across a suite of multi-stage, contact-rich dexterous manipulation tasks demonstrate that InDex effectively masters intricate skills with minimal demonstration data, substantially outperforming monolithic baselines while preserving the robust spatial generalizability of the original VLA prior.

items.summary: This paper presents our solution to the 2026 SoccerNet VQA Challenge. We first develop a cost-effective data synthesis pipeline driven by a Vision-Language Model (VLM), which systematically restructures raw domain data into diverse VQA samples, including concise answers and long-form responses. Second, we propose MSUE, a multi-expert question answering architecture that employs a Large Language Model (LLM) to dynamically dispatch questions to text, image, and video experts. These experts are instantiated as a strong text baseline Gemini3-Flash, a fine-tuned Qwen3-VL, and an external knowledge base, respectively, working collaboratively to enhance VQA performance. MSUE achieves an accuracy of \textbf{0.95} on the challenge benchmark, securing third place in the leaderboard.
items.author: Litao Li, Yibo Yu, Yufeng Hu, Zhuo Yang, Jiali Wen, Yixin Chen, Yixi Zhou

normalizedKeys: title:dam vla decoupled asynchronous multimodal vision language action model

items.title: DAM-VLA: Decoupled Asynchronous Multimodal Vision Language Action model
items.description: Vision-language-action (VLA) models inherit a shared synchronous clock from vision-language pretraining, processing every input at one rate. This is misaligned with physical interaction, where a high-frequency modality changes at hundreds of hertz, vision evolves more slowly, and language stays constant across an episode. A synchronous VLA oversamples slow modalities, undersamples fast ones, and caps action generation at the lowest effective frequency. We hypothesize that decoupling temporal pro
items.summary: Vision-language-action (VLA) models inherit a shared synchronous clock from vision-language pretraining, processing every input at one rate. This is misaligned with physical interaction, where a high-frequency modality changes at hundreds of hertz, vision evolves more slowly, and language stays constant across an episode. A synchronous VLA oversamples slow modalities, undersamples fast ones, and caps action generation at the lowest effective frequency. We hypothesize that decoupling temporal processing per modality, letting each update and retain information at its own sensor rate, yields stronger representations and more robust control. We present DAM-VLA, which maintains per-modality latent buffers refreshed at sensor rates and read continuously by the action head, integrating new high-frequency modalities through gated cross-attention that leaves the pretrained backbone intact. Across seven contact-rich real-world manipulation tasks, DAM-VLA more than doubles the average success rate of the strongest synchronous baseline (95.2\% vs.\ 40.95\%) while sustaining smooth, reactive 100\,Hz control. Project website: \href{https://intuitive-robots.github.io/DAM-VLA/}{intuitive-robots.github.io/DAM-VLA/}
items.author: Pankhuri Vanjani, Zhuoyue Li, Jakub Suliga, Moritz Reuss, Gianluca Geraci, Xinkai Jiang, Rudolf Lioutikov

trendEntities.title: Debiasing Without Protected Attributes: Latent Concept Erasure from Textual Profiles
normalizedKeys: title:debiasing without protected attributes latent concept erasure from textual profiles

items.title: Debiasing Without Protected Attributes: Latent Concept Erasure from Textual Profiles
items.description: Most fairness research in NLP assumes direct access to protected attributes such as gender, race, or nationality. In practice, however, such information is often unavailable due to privacy constraints, missing metadata, or legal restrictions, even though models may infer it from indirect textual cues. This raises a key question: can debiasing succeed without direct access to sensitive attributes? We propose H-SAL, which performs post-hoc concept and attribute erasure using self-description text
items.summary: Most fairness research in NLP assumes direct access to protected attributes such as gender, race, or nationality. In practice, however, such information is often unavailable due to privacy constraints, missing metadata, or legal restrictions, even though models may infer it from indirect textual cues. This raises a key question: can debiasing succeed without direct access to sensitive attributes? We propose H-SAL, which performs post-hoc concept and attribute erasure using self-description text as an implicit debiasing signal. To support this setting, we introduce a multi-domain Stack Exchange-based fairness benchmark for helpfulness prediction that includes both explicit and implicit signals, enabling comparison between standard debiasing with protected labels and debiasing without access to sensitive information. Across encoder and decoder-only language models, we find that implicit self-description often matches or outperforms explicit-label-based debiasing. Our results broaden representation-level fairness research and provide a new benchmark for studying debiasing under realistic data constraints.
items.author: Shun Shao, Zheng Zhao, Anna Korhonen, Yftah Ziser, Shay B. Cohen
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: benchmark

trendEntities.title: FORT-Searcher: Synthesizing Shortcut-Resistant Search Tasks for Training Deep Search Agents
normalizedKeys: title:fort searcher synthesizing shortcut resistant search tasks for training deep search agents

items.title: FORT-Searcher: Synthesizing Shortcut-Resistant Search Tasks for Training Deep Search Agents
items.description: Training deep search agents requires verifiable questions whose answers remain unavailable until sufficient evidence has been acquired through search. Existing synthesis methods often increase apparent difficulty by enriching graph structures, but structural complexity alone does not guarantee realized search difficulty: the intended search process can collapse through a cheaper identifying route. We formalize this gap with a shortcut-aware difficulty framework and identify four actionable short
items.summary: Training deep search agents requires verifiable questions whose answers remain unavailable until sufficient evidence has been acquired through search. Existing synthesis methods often increase apparent difficulty by enriching graph structures, but structural complexity alone does not guarantee realized search difficulty: the intended search process can collapse through a cheaper identifying route. We formalize this gap with a shortcut-aware difficulty framework and identify four actionable shortcut risks: evidence co-coverage, single-clue selectivity, exposed constants, and prior-knowledge binding. To diagnose their realized effects, we use trajectory signatures including solving cost, answer hit time, and prior-shortcut rate. Guided by this framework, we introduce FORT, a Framework of Shortcut-Resistant Training-Data Synthesis. FORT constructs shortcut-resistant training data by controlling shortcut risks across entity selection, evidence graph construction, question formulation, and adversarial refinement. Experiments show that FORT induces longer pre-answer search and fewer shortcut patterns than existing open-source deep search datasets. Using the resulting trajectories, we train FORT-Searcher with supervised fine-tuning (SFT) only, and it achieves the best overall performance among comparable-size open-source search agents on challenging deep search benchmarks. Relevant resources will be made available at https://github.com/RUCAIBox/FORT-Searcher.
items.author: Jia Deng, Yimeng Chen, Xiaoqing Xiang, Ziyang Zeng, Shuo Tang, Wayne Xin Zhao, Feng Chang, Chuan Hao, Yuan Wei, Ran Tao, Bryan Dai, Ji-Rong Wen
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag, fine-tuning, benchmark
